assertion_results.text: Events are added to local state with a 'pending' status before being sent to relays
assertion_results.evidence:
Found 10 match(es) for keyword "events" (heuristic match):

  optimistic-publisher.ts:65: *   - "rate-limited:" — too many events, backoff and retry

assertion_results.text: OK responses are tracked per-relay per-event (not just a single boolean)
assertion_results.evidence:
Found 10 match(es) for keyword "responses" (heuristic match):
  optimistic-publisher.ts:39: relayResponses: Map<string, RelayOkResponse>;
  optimistic-publisher.ts:232: private okTimeoutMs: number = 10000; // 10 second timeout for OK responses
  optimistic-publisher.ts:246: * 3. Track OK responses per relay

assertion_results.text: The 'duplicate:' OK reason prefix is treated as success (not failure)
Retriable error prefixes (rate-limited, auth-required) are distinguished from permanent failures (blocked, restricted)
assertion_results.evidence:
Found 10 match(es) for keyword "permanent" (heuristic match):
  optimistic-publisher.ts:7: * - OK reason prefix parsing (duplicate as success, retriable vs permanent errors)
  optimistic-publisher.ts:45: type OkReasonCategory = "success" | "retriable" | "permanent";
  optimistic-publisher.ts:70: * Permanent failure prefixes (don't retry):

assertion_results.text: Event status transitions to 'confirmed' when at least one relay accepts

assertion_results.text: Event status transitions to 'failed' only when ALL relays have rejected or timed out

assertion_results.text: Replaceable event deduplication keeps the latest created_at, and breaks ties by keeping the lowest event id
assertion_results.evidence:
Found 10 match(es) for keyword "replaceable" (heuristic match):
  optimistic-publisher.ts:10: * - Event deduplication for regular and replaceable events
  optimistic-publisher.ts:108: * Replaceable events (kinds 0, 3, 10000-19999): keep latest created_at,
  optimistic-publisher.ts:110: * Addressable events (kinds 30000-39999): same as replaceable but keyed
